message.content: {"post_number":7,"summary":"cjames23 says he would only standardize the interface if multiple widely adopted tools support it, noting hatch’s plugin system already covers most cases and that only a few plugins like zipped directory and hatch‑aws are truly distinct. He remains uncertain whether the feature will be widely used.","conversational_intent":"responding to feedback and discussing standardization","stance":"expressing uncertainty","technical_burden":"low","phrase_learning_potential":"high","important_uncertainty":"Uncertainty about whether the interface will be widely adopted and if non‑standard targets have sufficient demand."}
message.thinking:

The content: He says he wants more than one tool implementing something with significant adoption before standardizing. He notes that hatch provides plugin mechanism, but many plugins have narrow use cases. Exception: zipped directory plugin and hatch-aws. He also quotes MattP's earlier statement about frontends uv, hatch, pdm, etc. He expresses stance: uncertain about standardization, wants to see adoption. He is uncertain about demand. He is not making a claim. So stance: expressing uncertainty. Conversational intent: responding to feedback, discussing standardization. Technical burden: low. Phrase learning potential: high. He remains uncertain whether the feature will be widely used."
